data.change_summary: Eighth remediation round addressing 2 QA amendments: (1) Fixed TmuxAdapter incorrectly reporting enforces_timeout() == true. TmuxAdapter::wait_for_session_exit() is an infinite poll loop with no timeout enforcement — it relies entirely on the service-level timeout. Removed the enforces_timeout() override (reverts to default false), so the service uses request.timeout directly without the +30s buffer. Added a comment explaining the design rationale. (2) Fixed OpenRouterBackendAdapter::api_key() reporting a missing OPENROUTER_API_KEY as BackendUnavailable { failure_class: None }, which defaulted to TransportFailure (retryable) and burned 5 retry attempts. Changed to failure_class: Some(FailureClass::BinaryNotFound) to make it terminal, consistent with the same check in ProcessBackendAdapter::check_availability(). Added an explanatory comment.
outstanding_risks: Process group kill cannot be implemented due to #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe, and std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed on timeout/cancel.

The BinaryNotFound failure class name is used for both missing binaries and missing API keys. Doc comments clarify this dual use as a general 'fatal infrastructure prerequisite' signal, but operator logs will still show 'binary_not_found' for missing env vars. A future rename to FatalInfrastructure would be clearer but requires broader codebase changes.

The pre-existing flaky test service_emits_invocation_completed_trace_with_token_fields occasionally fails under high parallelism due to tracing subscriber interference between tests. This is unrelated to our changes and passes reliably in isolation.

steps.description: Removed enforces_timeout() override from TmuxAdapter: wait_for_session_exit() is an infinite poll loop with no inner timeout enforcement, so the adapter must return false (default) so the service-level timeout uses request.timeout directly without the +30s buffer. Added a comment explaining why TmuxAdapter does not enforce its own timeout. The BackendAdapter enum delegation still correctly calls adapter.enforces_timeout() which now returns the default false.

Changed OpenRouterBackendAdapter::api_key() at openrouter_backend.rs:70-75 to return BackendUnavailable { failure_class: Some(FailureClass::BinaryNotFound) } when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is missing or empty. This makes the missing API key error terminal (non-retryable), consistent with the same check in ProcessBackendAdapter::check_availability(). Added explanatory comment noting BinaryNotFound is used as a terminal infrastructure prerequisite signal.

Verified all 678 tests pass (cargo test), cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean, cargo fmt --check clean. Feature-gated: service_times_out_and_requests_backend_cancellation passes, all 7 tmux_adapter_test tests pass, availability_probe_requires_api_key_and_calls_models_endpoint passes.

validation_evidence: cargo test: 678 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored across all test binaries
cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
service_times_out_and_requests_backend_cancellation (--features test-stub): passes — confirms the service-level timeout still works correctly for non-timeout-enforcing adapters

All 7 tmux_adapter_test tests pass (--features test-stub): tmux_adapter_timeout_cleans_up_session_with_sigterm_then_sigkill confirms tmux timeout behavior is unchanged — the service-level timeout fires at request.timeout (not request.timeout + 30s)
availability_probe_requires_api_key_and_calls_models_endpoint: passes — existing test asserts BackendUnavailable for missing key, now with failure_class: Some(BinaryNotFound) making it terminal
All backend_diagnostics_test OpenRouterUnavailableAdapter tests pass — these test adapters use failure_class: None (simulating transient unavailability) and are unaffected by the api_key() change
